page: Microsoft Azure E64pds_v6 is an Epdsv6-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores based on the ARM64 architecture, utilizing the Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor clocked at 3.4 GHz. The server is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 3780 GB of local disk capacity, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. There are no GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses included.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core and memory performance. The server achieves top-tier ratings in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, and Geekbench multi-core scores. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, scoring in the top tier for both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached reads and writes. Passmark database operations and static web serving throughput also rank in the top tier. However, single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and Passmark CPU scores falling into the average category.

The ARM64 architecture lacks x86-specific instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512, AMX, and FMA, which limits its utility for workloads relying on these vector extensions. However, the dedicated physical cores eliminate hyper-threading resource contention, contributing to high multi-threaded efficiency. This resource density and high memory-to-vCPU ratio offer qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads. The server is suited for database operations, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and memory-bound data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E64pds_v6 is an ARM64-based virtual machine from the Epdsv6 family, featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processors running at 3.4 GHz. It is configured with 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.0 GB per core, and includes 3780 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. The server lacks hyper-threading and x86 vector extensions like AVX-512, but delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, as evidenced by top-tier scores in stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark database benchmarks. Single-core performance remains average. This memory-optimized profile, combined with high-capacity local storage, makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive and multi-threaded applications. It is suited for database hosting, large-scale software compilation, static web serving, and memory-bound data processing workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64pds_v6 is an ARM64 virtual server with 64 dedicated Neoverse-N2 cores, 512 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for database workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64pds_v6 is an ARM64 server with 64 dedicated cores, 512 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, optimized for database and memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated physical cores, high-capacity local storage, and top-tier multi-threaded memory bandwidth.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated ARM64 Neoverse-N2 physical cores running at 3.4 GHz
512 GB of RAM providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB
3780 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database benchmark performance
No hyper-threading or x86 vector extensions such as AVX or AMX
Suited for database hosting, multi-threaded compilation, and static web serving
